    If Mitchell is a Werewolf, he wants to divert votes to Justin.

    If Mike is the Robber, he might have robbed Justin.

    This is a very bold claim for a Werewolf to make unless they are trying to bluff as something else or are the Tanner. But the Tanner wants to be voted out. If Mitchell is the Tanner, he would want the votes. Instead, he's pushing votes toward Justin.

    Mitchell is admitting to being a Werewolf.

    In One Night Ultimate Werewolf, when someone admits to being a Werewolf, it's often a bluff (Tanner) or a very confused player. But Mitchell is actively trying to get Justin voted out.

parsed_output.justification: Justin is highly inconsistent in his claims, first stating he was the Troublemaker [21] and later claiming to be the Robber [102]. Mike also claimed to be the Robber [24], which makes Justin's second claim a likely lie. While Mitchell admits to starting as a Werewolf [76], this is an unusual move for a Werewolf and may be a bluff or a confusing play, whereas Justin's contradictions are more characteristic of a Werewolf attempting to deceive the group.

validation.justification: Justin is highly inconsistent in his claims, first stating he was the Troublemaker [21] and later claiming to be the Robber [102]. Mike also claimed to be the Robber [24], which makes Justin's second claim a likely lie. While Mitchell admits to starting as a Werewolf [76], this is an unusual move for a Werewolf and may be a bluff or a confusing play, whereas Justin's contradictions are more characteristic of a Werewolf attempting to deceive the group.
